publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-08-12 18:04:30 -0400.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Includes publications by Hartmanis and others in the field of computer science, correspondence, and subject files.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Emeritus professor of Computer Science, Cornell University.

Hartmanis was born in Latvia, received his Bachelor's degree from the University of Marburg in Germany, his Master's degree in Applied Mathematics from the University of Kansas City in 1951, and his Ph.D. in Mathematics from the California Institute of Technology in 1955. After teaching at Cornell and Ohio State, he joined the General Electric Research Laboratory in 1958, where he developed principles of computational complexity theory. In 1965 he became a professor at Cornell and was on of the founders and first chairman of its computer science department. In 1993, he won the Turing Award along with Richard Stearns.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Includes publications by Hartmanis and others in the field of computer science, correspondence, and subject files.

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series I. Papers by Various Computer Scientists Boxes 1-7; Box 8- Folders: 1-59
Series II. Reprints - Hartmanis Box 9- Folders: 1-66
Series III. Course Material Box 9- Folders: 66-88; Box 10- Folders: 1-9
Series IV. Correspondence Box 10- Folders: 10-20, 27-37; Box 11- Folder: 10
Series V. Lecture and Subject Files Box 10- Folders: 38-44; Box 11- Folders: 7-9, 11-50
Series VI. Department of Computer Science Material Box 10- Folders: 21-26; Box 21
Series VII. National Science Foundation (NSF) Grant Material Box 10- Folders: 45-47; Box 11- Folders: 1-6, 43, 47; Box 18
Series VIII. Other Professional Material Boxes 11-20

odd.head: General
odd.p: Additional material and digital versions can be found online here: http://ecommons.cornell.edu/handle/1813/14935

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Juris Hartmanis papers, #16-13-3786. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

Machine theory
Computer science
